sourceDescription: Bridge ranges inherited from verified constants already in production. NOT empirically derived from multi-season league data. Sources: scripts/generate-mlb-hr-props.mjs computeBatterHrScore fixed ranges (barrel, hard-hit, xBA, whiff, weather), the raw 0-100 pitcher hrVs composite scale, the generator DEFAULT_PARK_FACTORS table min/max (park), and src/pages/MlbHrProps.tsx buildHeatStatRanges display constants (last-7/last-30 HR, which production scores with same-slate min-max and therefore have no production fixed range).
populationDefinition: Current production fixed-range constants and checked-in tables; not a sampled league population. A future derivation script should replace this artifact with an empirical multi-season version under a new artifactVersion.

ranges.provenance: src/pages/MlbHrProps.tsx buildHeatStatRanges display constant { low: 0, high: 5 }. Production score uses same-slate min-max (normalizeMetric) — no production fixed range exists for this metric.
src/pages/MlbHrProps.tsx buildHeatStatRanges display constant { low: 0, high: 10 }. Production score uses same-slate min-max (normalizeMetric) — no production fixed range exists for this metric.
opposingPitcherHrVs is the hrVs composite from computePitcherMatchupRatings, already on a 0-100 scale and used raw (weight 0.15) in computeBatterHrScore.

Min/max of the checked-in DEFAULT_PARK_FACTORS table in generate-mlb-hr-props.mjs (0.85–1.40 across 30 venues). Production score uses same-slate min-max (normalizeMetric) — no production fixed range exists for this metric.
generate-mlb-hr-props.mjs computeBatterHrScore scaleToRange((weatherBoost ?? 0) + 10, 0, 20), i.e. a fixed [-10, +10] boost range; computeWeatherBoost clamps to that interval.
